vehicle.label: 6-DOF Industrial Arm
vehicle.desc: 6-DOF serial industrial robot arm, 5kg payload, 600mm reach

parts.label: Base Joint (J1)
parts.spec: Base rotary joint J1, +-180deg, harmonic drive, 100Nm peak torque, mounting flange ISO 9409

parts.label: Shoulder Joint (J2)
parts.spec: Shoulder pitch joint J2, +-120deg, harmonic drive, 80Nm, cable routing through center bore

parts.label: Upper Arm (Link 2-3)

parts.label: Elbow Joint (J3)
parts.spec: Elbow pitch joint J3, +-150deg, cycloidal reducer, 50Nm, encoder dual-stack

parts.label: Forearm + Wrist (J4)
parts.spec: Forearm with wrist roll J4, 250mm length, +-360deg continuous, slip ring for power/signal

parts.label: Wrist Pitch (J5)
parts.spec: Wrist pitch joint J5, +-120deg, harmonic drive, 20Nm, compact form factor

parts.label: Wrist Roll (J6)
parts.spec: Wrist final roll joint J6, +-360deg, harmonic drive, 10Nm, ISO 9409-1 50mm tool flange

parts.spec: Tool changer master plate at J6 end, pneumatic locking, 4 utility ports

parts.label: Cable Management Spine
parts.spec: External cable carrier from base to wrist, drag chain or spiral, signal + power + pneumatic

parts.label: Replaceable Motor Module Set

parts.label: Encoder Cover + Sensing Set
parts.spec: Sensing_encoders closure module: encoder_pocket and encoder_cover at each joint, index marks, cable exit strain relief, inspection labels

parts.label: Drive Electronics Bay
parts.spec: Drive_electronics bay with driver_bay, power_channel, signal_channel, ground_point, service cover, connector keying, thermal inspection window

risk.desc: Thermal dissipation fins may not be sufficient for high torque operation.
risk.mit: Increase the number or size of thermal dissipation fins.

risk.desc: Base plate may not have adequate stiffness for high torque loads.
risk.mit: Increase the thickness or material grade of the base plate.

risk.desc: Cable entry grommet may not provide sufficient strain relief.
risk.mit: Add additional strain relief features or larger grommet.

risk.desc: Interface flange may not have enough clearance for assembly.
risk.mit: Increase the clearance around the interface flange.

risk.desc: Drive shaft housing may not be robust enough for press fit.
risk.mit: Use a stronger material or design for a press fit with a larger clearance.

verify.rule: The blueprint must include all required fields and sections as per the schema.
The mass_est_g must be a numeric grams value and within the expected range for the subsystem.
Every internal_feature_target in P0 must appear as part_tree children and geometry_ops ids or targets.
Each adjacent_interface must name an exact neighboring subsystem label, choose a connection_type, DOF, clearance_mm, and required_feature so the neighbor can declare the same joint family back.
Critical joints need visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence and torque_Nm or preload intent.
Boundary_conditions must include fixture_points, thermal_zones, and pressure_faces with physically distributed coordinates.

risk.desc: The design does not explicitly account for electromagnetic interference (EMI) shielding around signal cables.
risk.mit: Add shielding materials or coatings to signal cables to mitigate EMI.

risk.desc: The pneumatic lines may experience pressure drops over the 600mm reach due to friction and bends.
risk.mit: Optimize the routing and diameter of pneumatic lines to minimize pressure loss.

risk.desc: The design lacks redundancy in the cable spine, which could lead to a single point of failure.
risk.mit: Implement a redundant cable spine or add fail-safe mechanisms.

risk.desc: The thermal dissipation design may not be sufficient for the entire reach of the arm.
risk.mit: Enhance the thermal management strategy, possibly by adding active cooling elements.

risk.desc: The design does not specify environmental sealing for the cable spine, which could lead to ingress of contaminants.
risk.mit: Include sealing materials or designs to protect the cable spine from environmental contaminants.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Cable carrier from base to wrist, drag chain or spiral, signal + power + pneumatic

physics_paths.description: Signal and power cables from base to wrist
Pneumatic lines from base to wrist
Thermal dissipation from cable spine along the arm

integration_opportunities.description: Battery module remains removable without full teardown
Harnesses are routed through visible raceways or protected channels
Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover
Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry and routing respects a minimum bend radius
Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling

risk.desc: The motor module housing may not be robust enough for the payload and joint torque requirements.
risk.mit: Conduct FEA analysis to verify structural integrity and adjust wall thickness or material as needed.

risk.desc: The cooling channel design may not be sufficient to dissipate heat from the motor module.
risk.mit: Increase the size of the cooling channel or add additional cooling features to improve heat dissipation.

risk.desc: The fastener pattern may not provide adequate preload for the joint coupling.
risk.mit: Review the fastener pattern and ensure it meets the required preload and torque specifications.

risk.desc: The service access panel may be difficult to reach for maintenance and inspection.
risk.mit: Ensure that the service access panel is easily accessible and that the assembly sequence allows for easy removal.

risk.desc: The harness routing path may be susceptible to strain and damage due to movement.
risk.mit: Add strain relief features and ensure the harness routing path avoids pinch points and sharp edges.

risk.desc: Risk of electromagnetic interference (EMI) due to unshielded components.
risk.mit: Implement shielding measures for critical components to reduce EMI.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Drive board bay, power/signal separation along the arm

physics_paths.path_description: Power lines run from the driver board to the motor, separated from signal lines
Signal lines from the encoder board to the control hub, routed through the signal channel
Thermal energy from the driver board is dissipated through the inspection window
Access for service and maintenance through the service cover and connector keying
